citations.narrative:
The current license authorizes a maximum of 50 total animals. During the inspection, 29 adult dogs and 33 puppies were
present, totaling 62 animals. USDA licenses authorize a maximum number of animals to ensure licensees have capacity
to provide for their care and well-being. Licensees must obtain a new license before any change resulting in more than
the authorized number of animals on hand at any single point in time during the period of licensure.
Correct by applying for a new license that authorizes an increased maximum number of animals that will be on hand at
any one time, including adult dogs and puppies, within the licensee's capacity to provide adequate care. Corrected at the
time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.

narrative:
2.1(b)(2)(i) 
   
Requirements and application 
The current license authorizes a maximum of 50 total animals. During the inspection, 29 adult dogs and 33 puppies were 
present, totaling 62 animals. USDA licenses authorize a maximum number of animals to ensure licensees have capacity 
to provide for their care and well-being. Licensees must obtain a new license before any change resulting in more than 
the authorized number of animals on hand at any single point in time during the period of licensure. 
 
Correct by applying for a new license that authorizes an increased maximum number of animals that will be on hand at 
any one time, including adult dogs and puppies, within the licensee's capacity to provide adequate care. Corrected at the 
time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the licensee.
